title: Dynamic responses to smoking bans: Evidence from young adults in a developing country

construct_vars: df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(id_chr = paste0(as.character(V0001), as.character(V0024), as.character(UPA_PNS), ifelse(nchar(as.character(V0006_PNS)) == 1, paste0('0', as.character(V0006_PNS)), as.character(V0006_PNS)), as.character(C00301)), uf = as.numeric(V0001), age_raw = as.numeric(C008), weight = as.numeric(V0029), capital = as.integer(V0031 == 1), smoker = as.numeric(P050), former_daily_smoker = as.numeric(P051), former_smoker = as.numeric(P052), starting_age = as.numeric(P053), years_quit = as.numeric(P05901), months_quit = as.numeric(P05902))

df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(regular_smoker = as.integer(smoker == 1), former_regular = as.integer(smoker == 3 & former_smoker == 1), smoking_time = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(starting_age), age_raw - starting_age, NA_real_), add_year = dplyr::case_when(!is.na(months_quit) & months_quit <= 6 ~ 0, !is.na(months_quit) & months_quit > 6 ~ 1, TRUE ~ NA_real_), quit_time = years_quit + add_year, stock = smoking_time - quit_time, mistake = as.integer(stock < 0))

df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(offset__ = 2013 - year, age = age_raw - offset__, smoke = as.integer((regular_smoker == 1 & !is.na(smoking_time) & smoking_time >= offset__) | (former_regular == 1 & !is.na(quit_time) & !is.na(smoking_time) & quit_time <= offset__ & smoking_time >= offset__)))

df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(id = dplyr::dense_rank(id_chr), t2008 = as.integer(uf == 11), t2009 = as.integer(uf %in% c(13, 14, 25, 35, 41, 52, 15, 28, 29, 12, 50, 33)), t2010 = as.integer(uf == 22), t2011 = as.integer(uf == 51))
df <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(Post_avg = as.integer(t2009 == 1 & year >= 2009), gvar_CS = dplyr::if_else(t2009 == 1, 2009, 0))
df <- df %>% dplyr::select(id, id_chr, uf, capital, weight, year, age, smoke, t2008, t2009, t2010, t2011, Post_avg, gvar_CS)

notes: Outcome selection protocol: main result chosen from Figure 3a (average effects on smoking prevalence). No explicit preferred specification among columns; same outcome and sample across variants, so protocol selects the first/main specification with the fewest time-varying controls. Implemented as a 2009-treated single-timing DiD after excluding 2008, 2010, and 2011 cohorts, matching the paper's baseline event-study sample. | AUDIT NOTE (2026-04-19): Null-result paper, robust across all estimators. TWFE=0.0024 (ns), CS-NT=0.0003 (ns). HonestDiD breakdown Mbar=0 for all targets (CIs include zero at every M level) — null doesn't depend on parallel-trend allowance. 0 controls → no Spec A/B/C decomposition possible. Paper-auditor NOT_APPLICABLE is legitimate: paper only reports generalized event-study with cohort-specific linear trends (Figure 3a); there is no scalar TWFE ATT to compare against. TWFE WARN is SYNTHETIC-PANEL SERIAL DEPENDENCE: PNS 2013 cross-section replicated × 10 years creates within-person correlation state-level clustering partially but not fully addresses. Limitation inherent to retrospective synthetic-panel design, acknowledged in paper. Pre-trend mild monotonic pattern t=-4→-1 (−0.007 → 0) does not flip null (HonestDiD robust). Single-cohort 2009 → Bacon and CS-NYT N/A.
